headline: <cite index="1-2,4-7">PE resurgence is gathering steam after 2025 saw buyout deals and exits surge to second-highest values on record.</cite>

stories.title: Beauty & Consumer Consolidation Accelerates: <cite index="63-1,63-13">Luxury fashion and beauty continue to show resilience through strategic acquisitions focused on brand consolidation and market expansion.</cite>
stories.summary: <cite index="61-3,61-4,61-5">The consumer market has entered unprecedented consolidation where PE firms deploy sophisticated aggregation strategies across fragmented sectors from beauty and personal care to specialty foods.</cite> Platforms unlock digital/data synergies that standalone brands can't afford.
stories.takeaway: DTC brands w/ strong customer data command 15-20% premium multiples; winners are scaling via bolt-on acquisitions & shared back-office functions.

stories.title: HVAC Services Roll-Ups Reach Peak Momentum: <cite index="75-12">PE add-on transactions targeting HVAC service providers rose 88% YoY through June, with PE firms & platforms combining for 39 of 77 HVAC M&A deals.</cite>
stories.summary: <cite index="71-3">PE firms remain dominant buyers of residential HVAC platforms, executing buy-and-build roll-ups across US regions while middle-market PE focuses on tuck-ins.</cite> <cite index="71-10">Valuations trade at premium with mid-teens EV/EBITDA for high-quality recurring revenue platforms.</cite>
stories.takeaway: Fragmented $350B+ market w/ 29K+ private companies attracts PE for maintenance-based recurring revenue & multitrade bundle (HVAC + plumbing + electrical) cross-sell opportunities.

stories.title: Healthcare PE Shifts to Value-Based Care: <cite index="59-6,59-8,59-9">Global healthcare PE reached record $191B in 2025, with firms increasingly investing in value-based care models emphasizing outcomes & cost efficiency.</cite>
stories.summary: <cite index="51-3,51-14">Tech-enabled care, behavioral health & physician specialty platforms see aggressive capital flows as acquirers scale models that grow without adding labor.</cite> <cite index="52-1">Margin expansion comes via AI-driven cost reduction.</cite>
stories.takeaway: Digital health platforms w/ clinical outcome data command premium valuations; clinical trial site consolidation (Baypine, Thomas H. Lee) heating up as PE pivot from physician practices.

stories.title: OneStream Take-Private Signals PE Bet on AI Finance: <cite index="41-4,41-5,41-6">Hg acquires OneStream for $6.4B in all-cash deal, taking it private 17 months after IPO, signaling strategic pivot to accelerate AI-driven roadmap away from quarterly public market pressures.</cite>
stories.summary: <cite index="41-2,41-13,41-14">OneStream reported $489.4M revenue (31% YoY growth) w/ subscription revenue growing 41%, positioning the unified Corporate Performance Management platform for next-gen finance transformation.</cite> <cite index="42-2">Absence of quarterly pressure & Hg's track record should positively impact OneStream's development.</cite>
stories.takeaway: Enterprise software valuations remain attractive at 13x revenue despite public market volatility; PE thesis centers on operational improvements & long-term innovation w/o quarterly earnings noise.

watchNext.title: <cite index="2-19">Firms standing out will be those finding or sharpening repeatable models for sourcing deals, determining early how to create value & executing at speed.</cite>
watchNext.why: New era demands operational excellence over multiple expansion; PE alpha now comes from disciplined value creation playbooks not financial engineering.

watchNext.title: <cite index="10-8,10-10,10-11">Entry multiples hit new high at 11.8x EBITDA in 2025; with lower leverage contribution, GPs emphasize value creation on revenue growth & margin expansion.</cite>
watchNext.why: Higher entry prices force PE to chase operational returns; <cite index="5-16">operators' ability to grow top-line revenue & improve margins increasingly under GP/LP scrutiny.</cite>
